pages.text: All Magic Storage blocks must connect by $(thing)direct adjacency$() - they must share a face (up, down, north, south, east, or west).$(br2)Cables or conduits are $(thing)not needed$(). Simply place blocks next to each other.

When you place or break any network block, the Core runs a $(thing)BFS (Breadth-First Search)$() to rediscover connected blocks. It scans outward up to $(thing)64 blocks$() in any direction.$(br2)The Core will skip past another Core it encounters, preventing network merging. Each Core maintains its own independent network.

$(thing)Pro tips:$()$(br2)$(li)You can build in any shape you like - lines, cubes, or sprawling webs.$(li)All network blocks (Units, Terminals, Buses) must be in the same chunk-forceloaded area for reliable operation.$(li)Breaking the Storage Core will detach the entire network, but items remain saved in the Core.
